evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Proactively monitor and govern data products to ensure high reliability. You will design and propose ongoing alerting frameworks to catch anomalies before they reach the business.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Repeated rigour signals — testing, CI/CD, governance, alerting, reliability, code review — dominate over the single velocity-adjacent phrase 'diving into the IDE to ship a critical fix'.
evidence.domain_risk: Finance domain knowledge, you understand how financial data works: P&L, FX, exposure, reconciliation.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Fintech company with explicit financial reporting/reconciliation language (P&L, FX, exposure), meeting the high override criteria.
evidence.data_team_maturity: We operate under a Domain-Driven Design (DDD) approach, where Product, Engineering, and Data align on shared goals
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A defined domain-ownership model and existing orchestration (Airflow/Cloud Composer) exist, but the role is asked to set standards within its domain from a position of ownership, indicating a growing (mid) rather than fully mature or greenfield function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Manage overall pipeline orchestration using Airflow (hosted in Cloud Composer).
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Airflow, Cloud Composer) and precise domain context (P&L, FX, reconciliation) make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Finance domain knowledge, you understand how financial data works: P&L, FX, exposure, reconciliation.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role is explicitly embedded in a finance-heavy fintech domain with P&L, FX, and reconciliation as core knowledge requirements.
evidence.autonomy_level: you are equally comfortable architecting a multi-domain roadmap as you are diving into the IDE to ship a critical fix
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role owns domain standards, architects roadmaps, and sets governance practices — a clearly strategic framing despite occasional hands-on execution.
evidence.ai_role: You will build the semantic layer that provides the context, definitions, and structure necessary for AI agents and business stakeholders to leverage data effectively
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds semantic infrastructure explicitly consumed by AI agents downstream, meeting the ai_enabler pattern (semantic modelling for AI).
evidence.testing_framing: Deliver hands-on data models using modern software engineering practices (version control, testing, and CI/CD).
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and CI/CD are framed as an owned engineering practice the candidate delivers and promotes, not just a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: design and propose ongoing alerting frameworks to catch anomalies before they reach the business
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: fintech context combined with explicit language about catching anomalies before they reach the business and building 'trusted, reusable assets' reflects fear of bad data reaching decision-makers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Lead projects from initial definition to delivery
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of setting new standards/building semantic layers (greenfield) and monitoring/governing existing data products for reliability (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
evidence.language_gate: Fluency in English (Spanish is a plus).
evidence.work_arrangement: Location: Madrid (Hybrid: 4 days office / 1 day WFH)
evidence.loss_aversion: catch anomalies before they reach the business — fintech domain data feeding financial reporting raises the cost of undetected errors.
